summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including the release of new AI models and tools. Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI and the EU's order for Google to open Android and Search to rivals are major developments. AI adoption in various industries, such as healthcare and policing, is also on the rise.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries raises important questions about data privacy, security, and ethics, highlighting the need for robust regulations and guidelines to ensure responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI alleges trade secret misappropriation and could impact OpenAI's IPO plans.
The EU has ordered Google to give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to Android and Google Search.
New AI models and tools, such as Kimi 3 and Gemini Notebook, are being released, showcasing rapid progress in AI technology.

articles.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
articles.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

articles.title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
articles.description: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
articles.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief

articles.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
articles.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done

articles.title: A scorecard for the AI age
articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
articles.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.
